pages.text: Three blocks and one mob that make the trading economy less tedious. Two work directly with villagers; the third with piglins; the fourth wanders the world on its own.
pages.title: Helpful Hands

pages.text: $(bold)The Trading Post.$() Place a $(italic)Trading Post$() block in the centre of your village. Right-click to open a unified GUI listing every nearby villager's trades — searchable by item, sortable by price. Click a trade and it executes against the villager who owns it; no more chasing the Librarian from his lectern to the gate and back again.$(br2)It is the single greatest quality-of-life improvement to vanilla trading.
pages.title: Trading Post

pages.text: $(bold)The Bartering Station.$() Place near piglins (overworld piglins from a converted zombified piglin will do; nether piglins in their native habitat work best). Drop $(bold)gold ingots$() in the input slot, and the piglins barter at the station rather than at you. Items pile up in the output slot.$(br2)The patient, no-running-around answer to gold-to-loot conversion.
pages.title: Bartering Station

pages.text: $(bold)Goblin Traders.$() $(italic)Goblin Traders$() are wandering green mobs that spawn rarely in caves and at the surface. Right-click one to trade — and their goods are $(italic)strange$(): potions of $(bold)Haste$(), $(bold)Absorption$(), and $(bold)Dolphin's Grace$() that exist nowhere else in the game, plus tools, redstone, food, and the occasional rarity.$(br2)The $(bold)Vein Goblin Trader$() spawns deeper underground, below Y=128, and deals in even stranger goods. A goblin will not despawn if named.
pages.title: Goblin Traders
